title: ambiguous column name when using multiple JOIN USING clauses and WHERE clause
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, age INT, email VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE);
CREATE TABLE orders (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, user_id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
CREATE TABLE products (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, price DECIMAL(10, 2) CHECK(price > 0));

CREATE TABLE order_items (id INT PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT, order_id INT, product_id INT, quantity INT CHECK(quantity > 0), FOREIGN KEY(order_id) REFERENCES orders(id), FOREIGN KEY(product_id) REFERENCES products(id));
SELECT u.name, o.amount FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE o.amount > (SELECT AVG(amount) FROM orders);
key_bug_features: multiple JOIN USING clauses with WHERE clause
